untranslatable_reason: This signal requires real-time order book microstructure data (best bid/offer levels, time precedence, aggressive buying/selling pressure detection) which cannot be expressed using only OHLCV-derived indicators. The core mechanics involve: (1) detecting price improvement opportunities relative to current best bid/ask (not available in OHLCV data), (2) measuring time precedence and order queue position (not available), (3) detecting when 'another trader shows interest' at improved prices (requires order book monitoring). A backtest engine operating on OHLCV bars cannot replicate the intra-bar microstructure dynamics and order priority logic central to this signal.
translation_notes: Signal describes a high-frequency market-making strategy based on order book positioning and time precedence. The parameters (hold_duration_seconds: 2, min_price_improvement_ticks: 1) and exit conditions (2 seconds elapsed, aggressive buying/selling pressure) require tick-level or order book data. The entry condition 'Quote entered and confirmed at improved price level' is an order action, not a price-based rule. Placeholder conditions use directional price movement as a proxy, but do not capture the true signal intent.
